title: Gas report - Week 37
summary:
LNG
It has been a steady week in the LNG market, with rates decreasing minimally across some routes as limited demand met steady vessel availability. The continued absence of fresh enquiry has left sentiment balanced as we move into mid/end October liftings. On the BLNG1 Australia-Japan route, 174k and 160k cbm vessels remained flat at $31,700 per day and $18,800 per day respectively.

children.title: LNG
children.summary: It has been a steady week in the LNG market, with rates decreasing minimally across some routes as limited demand met steady vessel availability. The continued absence of fresh enquiry has left sentiment balanced as we move into mid/end October liftings.

children.title: LPG
children.summary: It has been a firmer week in the LPG market, with rates rising sharply as a flurry of new cargoes entered the market and the tonnage list remained tight. The increased activity drove strong gains across all major routes.
